neural audio codecs."}', 'blog/neural-audio-codec-rvq', NULL, 'IZ0owy_BfWVwsBEoOkXEB4dH-9F1gU4UPXg7OfxvROk'); -- IZ0owy_BfWVwsBEoOkXEB4dH-9F1gU4UPXg7OfxvROk
INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/teaching-models-to-write-kernels.md', 'Teaching models to write kernels; dataset, training, and honest results', 'Anurag Kanade', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["p",{},"i wanted a model that actually understands low‑level GPU code: masked loads, boundary checks, shared memory... the stuff people sweat about and models usually mess up. this is a short, practical writeup: what i collected, how i cleaned it, how i trained, how i evaluated."],["hr",{}],["h2",{"id":"tldr"},"tl;dr"],["ul",{},["li",{},"a focused dataset of triton kernel bodies and cuda→triton translation pairs (a few thousand examples)."],["li",{},"cleaned, ",["a",{"href":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_deduplication","rel":["nofollow"]},"deduped"],", licensed... ready for adapter-style fine‑tuning."],["li",{},"i fine‑tuned adapters on a qwen 8b checkpoint using a single RTX 3090... results are useful as kernel drafts."]],["h2",{"id":"whats-in-the-dataset"},"what''s in the dataset"],["p",{},["strong",{},"HuggingFace Dataset:"]," ",["a",{"href":"https://huggingface.co/datasets/edwixx/triton-code-dataset","rel":["nofollow"]},"edwixx/triton-code-dataset"]],["p",{},["strong",{},"Fine-tuned Model:"]," ",["a",{"href":"https://huggingface.co/edwixx/qwen3-8b-triton-finetune","rel":["nofollow"]},"edwixx/qwen3-8b-triton-finetune"]],["p",{},"Two CSV splits, simple schema (",["code",{},"prompt,completion"],"):"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},["code",{},"fim_sft.csv"]]," — fill‑in‑the‑middle for triton kernels (prompt: signature + context, completion: body)."],["li",{},["strong",{},["code",{},"cu2triton_sft.csv"]]," — CUDA functions paired with hand‑crafted or curated Triton rewrites."]],["p",{},"Repo layout:"],["pre",{"className":["language-text"],"code":"/fim_sft.csv\n/cu2triton_sft.csv\n/ANALYSIS.json\n/ANALYSIS.md\n/PROVENANCE.md\n/figs/*.png\n","language":"text"},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},"/fim_sft.csv\n/cu2triton_sft.csv\n/ANALYSIS.json\n/ANALYSIS.md\n/PROVENANCE.md\n/figs/*.png\n"]],["hr",{}],["h2",{"id":"how-i-built-it"},"how i built it"],["p",{},"i started by searching repositories, documentation pages, and blog posts for kernel examples that show real GPU concerns. i prioritized permissively licensed sources and files that were self contained or had clear argument shapes."],["p",{},"next i normalized the text — unify newlines, trim whitespace, collapse repeated spaces and tabs:"],["pre",{"className":"language-python shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark","code":"def normalize_code(s: str) -> str:\n    import re\n    s = s.replace(''\\r\\n'', ''\\n'')\n    s = s.strip()\n    s = re.sub(r''[ \\t]+'', '' '', s)\n    s = re.sub(r''\\n{3,}'', ''\\n\\n'', s)\n    return s\n","language":"python","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{},"def normalize_code(s: str) -> str:\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{},"    import re\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":3},["span",{},"    s = s.replace(''\\r\\n'', ''\\n'')\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":4},["span",{},"    s = s.strip()\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":5},["span",{},"    s = re.sub(r''[ \\t]+'', '' '', s)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":6},["span",{},"    s = re.sub(r''\\n{3,}'', ''\\n\\n'', s)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":7},["span",{},"    return s\n"]]]],["p",{},"after cleaning i deduplicated exactly. compute a SHA‑1 hash over ",["code",{},"normalize(prompt) ||| normalize(completion)"]," and drop exact matches. then run a leakage filter using Jaccard word overlap and flag pairs with overlap ≥ 0.8 for manual review."],["hr",{}],["h2",{"id":"training"},"training"],["p",{},"i used qwen 8b and trained LoRA adapters on a single RTX 3090. loaded the base model with 4‑bit quantization using bitsandbytes."],["pre",{"className":"language-python shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark","code":"from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig\nfrom peft import LoraConfig\nfrom trl import SFTConfig\n\nbnb = BitsAndBytesConfig(\n    load_in_4bit=True,\n    bnb_4bit_quant_type=\"nf4\",\n    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=\"bfloat16\",\n)\n\npeft_cfg = LoraConfig(\n    r=32,\n    lora_alpha=32,\n    lora_dropout=0.05,\n    bias=\"none\",\n    target_modules=[\"q_proj\",\"k_proj\",\"v_proj\",\"o_proj\",\"gate_proj\",\"up_proj\",\"down_proj\"],\n    task_type=\"CAUSAL_LM\",\n)\n\ncfg = SFTConfig(\n    output_dir=\"qwen3_8b_triton_fim_lora\",\n    num_train_epochs=1,\n    per_device_train_batch_size=1,\n    gradient_accumulation_steps=16,\n    learning_rate=1e-4,\n    lr_scheduler_type=\"cosine\",\n    warmup_ratio=0.02,\n    packing=True,\n    max_length=1024,\n    bf16=True,\n    gradient_checkpointing=True,\n)\n","language":"python","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{},"from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{},"from peft import LoraConfig\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":3},["span",{},"from trl import SFTConfig\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":4},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":5},["span",{},"bnb = BitsAndBytesConfig(\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":6},["span",{},"    load_in_4bit=True,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":7},["span",{},"    bnb_4bit_quant_type=\"nf4\",\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":8},["span",{},"    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=\"bfloat16\",\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":9},["span",{},")\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":10},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":11},["span",{},"peft_cfg = LoraConfig(\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":12},["span",{},"    r=32,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":13},["span",{},"    lora_alpha=32,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":14},["span",{},"    lora_dropout=0.05,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":15},["span",{},"    bias=\"none\",\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":16},["span",{},"    target_modules=[\"q_proj\",\"k_proj\",\"v_proj\",\"o_proj\",\"gate_proj\",\"up_proj\",\"down_proj\"],\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":17},["span",{},"    task_type=\"CAUSAL_LM\",\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":18},["span",{},")\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":19},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":20},["span",{},"cfg = SFTConfig(\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":21},["span",{},"    output_dir=\"qwen3_8b_triton_fim_lora\",\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":22},["span",{},"    num_train_epochs=1,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":23},["span",{},"    per_device_train_batch_size=1,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":24},["span",{},"    gradient_accumulation_steps=16,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":25},["span",{},"    learning_rate=1e-4,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":26},["span",{},"    lr_scheduler_type=\"cosine\",\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":27},["span",{},"    warmup_ratio=0.02,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":28},["span",{},"    packing=True,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":29},["span",{},"    max_length=1024,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":30},["span",{},"    bf16=True,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":31},["span",{},"    gradient_checkpointing=True,\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":32},["span",{},")\n"]]]],["hr",{}],["h2",{"id":"evaluation"},"evaluation"],["p",{},"i kept eval simple — mix of automated and manual:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"static checks:"]," grep for ",["code",{},"@triton.jit"],", ",["code",{},"tl.load"],", masked stores. fast signal."],["li",{},["strong",{},"token overlap:"]," for translation pairs, measure how much the output matches reference."],["li",{},["strong",{},"spot compile:"]," try compiling a few examples. catches syntax errors."],["li",{},["strong",{},"human read:"]," i read ~100 outputs. catches weird patterns machines miss."]],["hr",{}],["h2",{"id":"example-the-model-learned"},"example the model learned"],["p",{},["strong",{},"prompt"]],["pre",{"className":"language-python shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark","code":"import triton, triton.language as tl\n\n@triton.jit\ndef add_kernel(x_ptr, y_ptr, out_ptr, n, BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr):\n    # complete: load x,y (masked), add, store\n","language":"python","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{},"import triton, triton.language as tl\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":3},["span",{},"@triton.jit\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":4},["span",{},"def add_kernel(x_ptr, y_ptr, out_ptr, n, BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr):\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":5},["span",{},"    # complete: load x,y (masked), add, store\n"]]]],["p",{},["strong",{},"completion"]],["pre",{"className":"language-python shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark","code":"pid = tl.program_id(0)\noffsets = pid * BLOCK_SIZE + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)\nmask = offsets < n\nx = tl.load(x_ptr + offsets, mask=mask)\ny = tl.load(y_ptr + offsets, mask=mask)\nout = x + y\ntl.store(out_ptr + offsets, out, mask=mask)\n","language":"python","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{},"pid = tl.program_id(0)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{},"offsets = pid * BLOCK_SIZE + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":3},["span",{},"mask = offsets < n\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":4},["span",{},"x = tl.load(x_ptr + offsets, mask=mask)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":5},["span",{},"y = tl.load(y_ptr + offsets, mask=mask)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":6},["span",{},"out = x + y\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":7},["span",{},"tl.store(out_ptr + offsets, out, mask=mask)\n"]]]],["p",{},"This pattern repeats across kernels; a few dozen examples are enough for the model to reproduce it reliably."],["hr",{}],["h2",{"id":"limitations"},"limitations"],["ul",{},["li",{},"small dataset: teaches idioms, not everything. expect hallucinations."],["li",{},"translations may not compile in edge cases. treat outputs as drafts."],["li",{},"no automatic proof of correctness across all outputs."]],["hr",{}],["h2",{"id":"next-steps"},"next steps"],["ul",{},["li",{},"add fuzzy near‑duplicate detection (minhash) to cut semantic leakage."],["li",{},"run compile tests for a subset and label examples as \"compilable.\""]],["hr",{}],["p",{},"PS: i did all this just cause i wanted to — this was a fun project, nothing more."],["style",{},"html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}"]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[{"id":"tldr","depth":2,"text":"tl;dr"},{"id":"whats-in-the-dataset","depth":2,"text":"what''s in the dataset"},{"id":"how-i-built-it","depth":2,"text":"how i built it"},{"id":"training","depth":2,"text":"training"},{"id":"evaluation","depth":2,"text":"evaluation"},{"id":"example-the-model-learned","depth":2,"text":"example the model learned"},{"id":"limitations","depth":2,"text":"limitations"},{"id":"next-steps","depth":2,"text":"next steps"}]}}', '["Machine Learning","GPU","Triton","CUDA"]', '2025-10-29', 'A practical guide to building a focused dataset and fine-tuning models to understand low-level GPU code - masked loads, boundary checks, shared memory, and CUDA→Triton translations.', 'md', NULL, '{}', 'true', '/blog/teaching-models-to-write-kernels', '{"title":"Teaching models to write kernels; dataset, training, and honest results","description":"A practical guide to building a focused dataset and fine-tuning models to understand low-level GPU code - masked loads, boundary checks, shared memory, and CUDA→Triton translations."}', 'blog/teaching-models-to-write-kernels', NULL, 'MAIkJ9W_QOPCoWdg4ukOtLjeT92rpozVzYRtpOe09Tw'); -- MAIkJ9W_QOPCoWdg4ukOtLjeT92rpozVzYRtpOe09Tw
INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/tpu-silicon-engine-ai-training.md', 'Tensor Processing Units (TPUs): The Silicon Engine Behind Modern AI Training', 'Anurag Kanade', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["p",{},"Every neural network training run burns through billions of matrix multiplications. Doing that on standard processors was never going to scale."],["p",{},"Now imagine if a chip was purpose-built for the exact math that powers deep learning. That idea, realized in Tensor Processing Units, changed how the world trains large models."],["h2",{"id":"the-challenge-behind-training-at-scale"},"The Challenge Behind Training at Scale"],["p",{},"Modern AI systems face a fundamental bottleneck: training a large language model like BERT or GPT requires processing trillions of floating-point operations. Multiply that by the need for faster experimentation cycles, and compute quickly becomes the limiting factor. Earlier approaches like CPU clusters or even GPU farms helped, but each involved trade-offs — throwing more hardware at the problem at the cost of power consumption, cost, or programming complexity."],["h2",{"id":"understanding-the-tpu-advantage"},"Understanding the TPU Advantage"],["p",{},"Before we jump into how TPUs accelerate training, it helps to understand what makes them different from GPUs. In machine learning, the dominant operations are ",["strong",{},"matrix multiplications"]," — massive batches of multiply-accumulate operations that transform high-dimensional tensors."],["p",{},"GPUs were originally designed for graphics: rendering pixels, shading triangles, parallel texture mapping. They happen to be good at deep learning because graphics and neural networks both benefit from parallel computation. But GPUs are general-purpose parallel processors."],["blockquote",{},["p",{},["strong",{},"NOTE:"]," TPUs are not just \"faster GPUs.\" They are ",["a",{"href":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Application-specific_integrated_circuit","rel":["nofollow"]},"application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs)"]," designed exclusively for the tensor operations that dominate neural network training — trading generality for raw efficiency in a specific domain."]],["p",{},"This makes them incredibly efficient for model training, but it''s a trade-off: TPUs excel at tensor math but are less flexible for arbitrary computations."],["h2",{"id":"matrix-multiply-units-mxu"},"Matrix Multiply Units (MXU)"],["p",{},"Inside the TPU resides the ",["strong",{},"Matrix Multiply Unit (MXU)"],", a massive ",["a",{"href":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Systolic_array","rel":["nofollow"]},"systolic array"]," that performs tens of thousands of operations per clock cycle."],["p",{},["a",{"href":"https://docs.cloud.google.com/tpu/docs/v4","rel":["nofollow"]},"TPU v4"]," can perform ",["strong",{},"275 trillion"]," matrix multiply-accumulate operations per second. To understand why this matters, let''s look at a typical BERT-large layer:"],["pre",{"className":["language-text"],"code":"Operations = 32 × 1024 × 4096 = 134 million MACs per layer forward pass\n","language":"text"},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},"Operations = 32 × 1024 × 4096 = 134 million MACs per layer forward pass\n"]],["p",{},"And that''s just one layer. BERT-large has 24 of them, plus backward passes, plus multiple training steps."],["pre",{"className":"language-python shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark","code":"# Input: batch of 32 sequences, each with 1024-dimensional embeddings\nX = np.random.randn(32, 1024).astype(np.float32)\n\n# Weights: projecting from 1024 to 4096 dimensions\nW = np.random.randn(1024, 4096).astype(np.float32)\n\nb = np.zeros(4096, dtype=np.float32)\n\n# Standard matrix multiplication: 134M operations\nY = np.matmul(X, W) + b\n\nprint(f\"Operations: {32 * 1024 * 4096:,} multiply-accumulates\")\n","language":"python","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{},"# Input: batch of 32 sequences, each with 1024-dimensional embeddings\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{},"X = np.random.randn(32, 1024).astype(np.float32)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":3},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":4},["span",{},"# Weights: projecting from 1024 to 4096 dimensions\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":5},["span",{},"W = np.random.randn(1024, 4096).astype(np.float32)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":6},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":7},["span",{},"b = np.zeros(4096, dtype=np.float32)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":8},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":9},["span",{},"# Standard matrix multiplication: 134M operations\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":10},["span",{},"Y = np.matmul(X, W) + b\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":11},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":12},["span",{},"print(f\"Operations: {32 * 1024 * 4096:,} multiply-accumulates\")\n"]]]],["p",{},"On a CPU, this executes sequentially. On a GPU, it runs in parallel across CUDA cores. On a TPU, the entire operation flows through a systolic array in a pipelined fashion."],["h2",{"id":"systolic-arrays"},"Systolic Arrays"],["p",{},"A ",["strong",{},"systolic array"]," is a 2D grid of processing elements where each element:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Receives input from its neighbors"],["li",{},"Performs a multiply-accumulate operation (MAC)"],["li",{},"Passes results to the next element"]],["p",{},["img",{"alt":"Systolic Array for Matrix Multiplication","src":"https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/13/Output_Stationary_Systolic_Array_Example.png/960px-Output_Stationary_Systolic_Array_Example.png"}]],["p",{},["em",{},"Figure: Systolic Array for Matrix Multiplication. Source: ",["a",{"href":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Systolic_array","rel":["nofollow"]},"Wikipedia"]]],["p",{},"This pipelined approach means once the array is filled, it produces one result element per clock cycle with minimal memory access."],["h2",{"id":"tpu-memory-high-bandwidth-on-chip"},"TPU Memory: High Bandwidth, On-Chip"],["p",{},"Traditional architectures suffer from the ",["strong",{},"von Neumann bottleneck"],": moving data between memory and compute units takes time and energy. TPUs address this with:"],["table",{},["thead",{},["tr",{},["th",{},"Component"],["th",{},"Specification"]]],["tbody",{},["tr",{},["td",{},"HBM (High Bandwidth Memory)"],["td",{},"Up to 32 GB with 1200 GB/s bandwidth per TPU v4 chip"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"On-chip SRAM"],["td",{},"144 MB of ultra-fast scratchpad memory"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"Communication links"],["td",{},"Dedicated high-speed links for all-reduce operations"]]]],["h2",{"id":"limitations-of-traditional-accelerators"},"Limitations of Traditional Accelerators"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Memory bandwidth bound"]," — When fetching data takes longer than computing, the processor sits idle. GPUs often achieve only 30–50% of peak compute utilization."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Kernel launch overhead"]," — Each CUDA kernel launch has ~5–10 microseconds of overhead."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Limited on-chip memory"]," — GPU shared memory (~100KB per SM) requires frequent off-chip accesses for large models."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Communication bottlenecks"]," — All-reduce operations for gradient synchronization become the bottleneck as cluster size grows."]],["p",{},"TPUs address these by co-designing hardware and software — XLA fuses operations, minimizes memory transfers, and optimizes the entire compute graph for the MXU architecture."],["h2",{"id":"xla-the-compiler-that-unlocks-tpu-performance"},"XLA: The Compiler That Unlocks TPU Performance"],["p",{},["strong",{},["a",{"href":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/XLA_(software)","rel":["nofollow"]},"XLA (Accelerated Linear Algebra)"]]," takes your TensorFlow/PyTorch/JAX code and:"],["ol",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Fuses operations"]," — Combines multiple ops into single kernels"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Eliminates intermediate allocations"]," — Reduces memory traffic"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Optimizes layouts"]," — Arranges tensors for efficient MXU access"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Automatic parallelism"]," — Distributes computation across TPU cores"]],["pre",{"className":"language-python shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark","code":"import tensorflow as tf\n\n@tf.function(jit_compile=True)\ndef dense_layer(x, w, b):\n    \"\"\"XLA will fuse matmul + add + activation into a single kernel\"\"\"\n    return tf.nn.gelu(tf.matmul(x, w) + b)\n","language":"python","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{},"import tensorflow as tf\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{"emptyLinePlaceholder":true},"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":3},["span",{},"@tf.function(jit_compile=True)\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":4},["span",{},"def dense_layer(x, w, b):\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":5},["span",{},"    \"\"\"XLA will fuse matmul + add + activation into a single kernel\"\"\"\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":6},["span",{},"    return tf.nn.gelu(tf.matmul(x, w) + b)\n"]]]],["p",{},"On TPU, this runs as one optimized operation instead of three separate kernels."],["h2",{"id":"tpu-pods-scaling-beyond-single-chips"},"TPU Pods: Scaling Beyond Single Chips"],["p",{},"TPU v4 pods contain up to ",["strong",{},"4,096 chips"]," interconnected with high-speed optical switches, enabling:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Model parallelism"]," — Splitting giant models across chips"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Data parallelism"]," — Processing different batches on different chips"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Pipeline parallelism"]," — Staggering computation across layers"]],["p",{},"The all-reduce bandwidth of 300 GB/s per chip means gradient synchronization stays efficient even with thousands of chips."],["h2",{"id":"key-optimizations-for-tpu-training"},"Key Optimizations for TPU Training"],["h3",{"id":"batch-size"},"Batch Size"],["p",{},"On TPU, batch size should be a multiple of 8 (TPU v2/v3) or 4 (TPU v4) times the number of TPU cores:"],["pre",{"className":["language-text"],"code":"Optimal Batch Size = k × num_cores × MXU_width\n","language":"text"},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},"Optimal Batch Size = k × num_cores × MXU_width\n"]],["h3",{"id":"use-bfloat16"},"Use bfloat16"],["p",{},"TPUs natively support ",["strong",{},"bfloat16"],", maintaining the dynamic range of float32 while using half the memory:"],["pre",{"className":"language-python shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark","code":"tf.keras.mixed_precision.set_global_policy(''mixed_bfloat16'')\n","language":"python","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{},"tf.keras.mixed_precision.set_global_policy(''mixed_bfloat16'')\n"]]]],["h2",{"id":"tpu-generations"},"TPU Generations"],["table",{},["thead",{},["tr",{},["th",{},"Generation"],["th",{},"Year"],["th",{},"Peak Performance (BF16)"],["th",{},"Memory"]]],["tbody",{},["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v1"],["td",{},"2016"],["td",{},"92 TOPS"],["td",{},"8 GB HBM"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v2"],["td",{},"2017"],["td",{},"180 TFLOPS"],["td",{},"16 GB HBM"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v3"],["td",{},"2018"],["td",{},"420 TFLOPS"],["td",{},"32 GB HBM"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v4"],["td",{},"2021"],["td",{},"275 TFLOPS/chip"],["td",{},"32 GB HBM"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v5e"],["td",{},"2023"],["td",{},"197 TFLOPS"],["td",{},"16 GB HBM"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v5p"],["td",{},"2023"],["td",{},"459 TFLOPS"],["td",{},"95 GB HBM"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v6e (Trillium)"],["td",{},"2024"],["td",{},"918 TFLOPS/chip"],["td",{},"32 GB HBM"]],["tr",{},["td",{},"TPU v7x (Ironwood)"],["td",{},"2025"],["td",{},"2,307 TFLOPS/chip"],["td",{},"192 GB HBM"]]]],["h2",{"id":"references"},"References"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Jouppi, N. P., et al."]," (2017). ",["em",{},"In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a Tensor Processing Unit"],". ",["a",{"href":"https://arxiv.org/abs/1704.04760","rel":["nofollow"]},"arXiv:1704.04760"]],["li",{},["strong",{},"Google Cloud."]," ",["a",{"href":"https://cloud.google.com/tpu/docs","rel":["nofollow"]},"TPU Documentation"]],["li",{},["strong",{},"TensorFlow."]," ",["a",{"href":"https://www.tensorflow.org/guide/tpu","rel":["nofollow"]},"TPU Strategy Guide"]],["li",{},["strong",{},"JAX Documentation."]," ",["a",{"href":"https://jax.readthedocs.io/en/latest/jax-101/08-pjit.html","rel":["nofollow"]},"Using JAX on TPU"]]],["style",{},"html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}"]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[{"id":"the-challenge-behind-training-at-scale","depth":2,"text":"The Challenge Behind Training at (MXU)"},{"id":"systolic-arrays","depth":2,"text":"Systolic Arrays"},{"id":"tpu-memory-high-bandwidth-on-chip","depth":2,"text":"TPU Memory: High Bandwidth, On-Chip"},{"id":"limitations-of-traditional-accelerators","depth":2,"text":"Limitations of Traditional Accelerators"},{"id":"xla-the-compiler-that-unlocks-tpu-performance","depth":2,"text":"XLA: The '/blog/tpu-silicon-engine-ai-training', '{"title":"Tensor Processing Units (TPUs): The Silicon Engine Behind Modern AI Training","description":"Exploring TPUs and why they''re so effective for large-scale AI training."}', 'blog/tpu-silicon-engine-ai-training', NULL, 'Ca8_XstfJcVnLJSHdm-vDd9v26FIZfbO-mq1_raMDhU'); -- Ca8_XstfJcVnLJSHdm-vDd9v26FIZfbO-mq1_raMDhU
INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/tts-datasets.md', 'Building TTS Datasets That Actually Work', 'Anurag Kanade', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["p",{},"Let''s get one thing straight: your TTS model will be as good as the data you train it upon. You can use the best pre-trained SOTA models out there, but without a high-quality dataset, you can''t get the model to speak naturally and lifelike."],["p",{},"TTS (Text-to-Speech) models require a dataset with audio files and their respective transcription. The main advantage of TTS architecture is that you don''t need to align the text transcriptions to the audio because the model can grasp the alignment process."],["h2",{"id":"popular-datasets-for-tts"},"Popular Datasets for TTS"],["p",{},"If you''re just starting out or want to see how others have done it, here are some datasets worth looking at:"],["h3",{"id":"public-datasets-everyone-uses"},"Public datasets everyone uses"],["ul",{},["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://keithito.com/LJ-Speech-Dataset/","rel":["nofollow"]},"LJSpeech"]," — single speaker, clean audio, very popular"],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://www.openslr.org/60/","rel":["nofollow"]},"LibriTTS"]," — multi-speaker dataset derived from LibriVox audiobooks"],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/bryanpark/the-world-english-bible-speech-dataset","rel":["nofollow"]},"TWEB"]," — The World English Bible speech dataset"]],["h3",{"id":"my-own-datasets"},"My own datasets"],["p",{},"I''ve also built some TTS datasets that you might find useful, especially for non-English languages or specific use cases:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://huggingface.co/datasets/edwixx/Gujrati_Female_SPeech","rel":["nofollow"]},"Gujarati Female Speech"]," — 8 hours of clean single-speaker Gujarati audio. Recorded in controlled conditions with aligned transcripts."],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://huggingface.co/datasets/edwixx/brazilian-portuguese-TTS","rel":["nofollow"]},"Brazilian Portuguese TTS"]," — ~150 hours of multi-speaker Brazilian Portuguese, covering different accents and speaking styles, normalized and ready to train."],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://huggingface.co/datasets/ModelsLab/Obama-Sample-Dataset","rel":["nofollow"]},"Obama Voice Sample Dataset"]," — 25+ minutes of Barack Obama''s voice from public speeches, optimized for RVC training. Clean 24 kHz WAV files."]],["p",{},"Check out more on the ",["a",{"href":"/datasets"},"datasets page"],"."],["h2",{"id":"things-to-consider-while-building-the-dataset"},"Things to Consider While Building the Dataset"],["h3",{"id":"noise-free"},"Noise-free"],["p",{},"Make sure the audio samples are noise-free. Background noise may lead your model not to learn well, and ultimately won''t be able to learn good alignment. Even if it learns alignment, the final output will be much worse than anticipated."],["h3",{"id":"consistency"},"Consistency"],["p",{},"Audio samples within your dataset should have the same format (mp3, flac, opus, wav) and sampling rate — ideally between 16kHz–22kHz. If you have high-quality audio with a higher sampling rate, normalize the configs."],["h3",{"id":"naturalness"},"Naturalness"],["p",{},"Your model will learn what samples you feed into it. If you expect a natural-sounding voice with speed, pitch, and intonation differences, the dataset should accommodate the same."],["h3",{"id":"diverse-phonemes"},"Diverse Phonemes"],["p",{},"Make sure your dataset covers a good set of phonemes for your use case. If phoneme coverage is low, the model will struggle to pronounce certain words."],["h3",{"id":"correctness"},"Correctness"],["p",{},"Before training, filter out bad-quality transcripts, compare transcript and audio lengths, and remove wrong or broken files."],["h3",{"id":"clip-length-distribution"},"Clip Length Distribution"],["p",{},"Verify the distribution of clip lengths and make sure your dataset has sufficient short and long audio clips. At maximum, use 30-second clips based on your available compute."],["h2",{"id":"additional-quality-checks"},"Additional Quality Checks"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Check the spectrogram of audio files to measure noise levels. If the spectrogram looks cluttered in silent parts, the dataset might not be good for training."],["li",{},"Analyze the dataset distribution in terms of clip and transcript lengths. Watch for outlier cases — a very long clip with short text, or a short clip with very long text, which can happen when using models like Whisper for transcription."]]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[{"id":"popular-datasets-for-tts","depth":2,"text":"Popular Datasets for TTS","children":[{"id":"public-datasets-everyone-uses","depth":3,"text":"Public datasets everyone uses"},{"id":"my-own-datasets","depth":3,"text":"My own datasets"}]},{"id":"things-to-consider-while-building-the-dataset","depth":2,"text":"Things to Consider While Building the Dataset","children":[{"id":"noise-free","depth":3,"text":"Noise-free"},{"id":"consistency","depth":3,"text":"Consistency"},{"id":"naturalness","depth":3,"text":"Naturalness"},{"id":"diverse-phonemes","depth":3,"text":"Diverse Phonemes"},{"id":"correctness","depth":3,"text":"Correctness"},{"id":"clip-length-distribution","depth":3,"text":"Clip Length Distribution"}]},{"id":"additional-quality-checks","depth":2,"text":"Additional Quality Checks"}]}}', '["Datasets","TTS","Data"]', '2025-06-11', 'your model is only as good as your data - here''s what you need to know about building high-quality TTS datasets', 'md', NULL, '{}', 'true', '/blog/tts-datasets', '{"title":"Building TTS Datasets That Actually Work","description":"your model is only as good as your data - here''s what you need to know about building high-quality TTS datasets"}', 'blog/tts-datasets', NULL, 'Bbre6EnGJkWA36EMCirPZFmevLs3qMulnrPBbqtFNNI'); -- Bbre6EnGJkWA36EMCirPZFmevLs3qMulnrPBbqtFNNI
